label: Graylog
description: Connect to Graylog to search logs, manage streams, and trigger alerts from your log management platform.

form.title: Connect Graylog
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to query and manage your Graylog log management instance.

methods.label: API Access Token
methods.description: Paste your Graylog API URL and an access token generated from System → Authentication → Tokens. Simplest and most secure path — no password stored.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Graylog Access Token

Graylog supports long-lived access tokens that can be used in place of a username and password. This is the recommended method.

### Steps

1. Log in to your Graylog web interface.
2. Navigate to **System → Authentication → Tokens** (or click your username in the top-right corner → **Edit profile → Tokens**).
3. Click **Create token**, give it a descriptive name like `Anton`, and click **Create**.
4. Copy the token value shown — it is only displayed once.
5. Find your Graylog API base URL. This is typically the same host and port as the web interface, e.g. `https://graylog.yourcompany.com:9000`. If your admin has configured a separate API endpoint, use that instead.
6. Paste the base URL into **Graylog API URL** and the token into **Access Token** below.

### Notes

- The token is used as a username with the literal string `token` as the password via HTTP Basic Auth — Anton handles this automatically.
- Tokens can be revoked at any time from the same **Tokens** page.
- If the Tokens page is missing, your Graylog version or role may not support it — use **Username & Password** instead.

fields.name: api_url
fields.label: Graylog API URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Graylog instance, including protocol and port. No trailing slash needed.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Access Token
fields.description: Generated from System → Authentication → Tokens (or your profile page). Shown only once at creation.

methods.label: Username & Password
methods.description: Authenticate with your Graylog username and password directly. Useful if you haven't set up access tokens or are using a local admin account.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Username & Password

Use this method if you prefer to authenticate with your existing Graylog credentials rather than generating a token.

### Steps

1. Find your Graylog API base URL — typically the same host and port as the web interface, e.g. `https://graylog.yourcompany.com:9000`.
2. Enter the username you use to log in to Graylog (e.g. `admin`).
3. Enter your Graylog password.
4. Click **Submit**. Anton will use HTTP Basic Auth to authenticate against the Graylog REST API.

### Notes

- For security, prefer the **API Access Token** method so you don't store your main account password.
- If your Graylog instance uses LDAP or SSO, username/password may not work via the API — generate a token instead.
- Ensure the account has sufficient role permissions (at minimum **Reader** for log search, **Admin** for full access).

fields.name: api_url
fields.label: Graylog API URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Graylog instance, including protocol and port. No trailing slash needed.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Your Graylog login password. Consider using an access token instead for better security.
